title: Product Manager, YouTube Create
role_summary: PM for YouTube Create — a consumer-facing creator tool product within YouTube, focused on enabling content creation at scale.
why_good_fit: Jay's creator economy experience is directly transferable: he scaled Resso's creator-facing features, led TikTok SoundOn music distribution, built the Ghost Music Tab for artist activation, and worked on Meta's content relevance and creator growth initiatives. His deep understanding of Indian regional/long-tail creators and Tier 2/3 user behaviour (Resso's core thesis of targeting users ignored by Spotify) maps perfectly to YouTube Create's mission of democratising creation tools. His Shoppertainment work at Meta shows he understands the creator-commerce convergence. 5 years experience requirement is well within Jay's 7-year range.
how_to_win: Lead with the Resso founding story — scaling a creation/consumption platform from alpha to India's largest music streaming app. Emphasise the 10%/90% content insight that shaped recommendation strategy, showing product intuition for creator tools. Connect Meta's creator growth work and GenZ Product Council membership to YouTube Create's audience. Pitch the insight that Indian creators need vernacular-first, mobile-first creation tools — cite field work observations. Show AI fluency as a differentiator for building AI-powered creation features.
job_description: You'll own the product roadmap for YouTube Create, a suite of tools that helps creators produce, edit, and publish content on YouTube. Day-to-day involves defining features that lower the barrier to content creation, working with engineering and design teams to ship creator-facing products, and measuring success through creator adoption, content output, and engagement metrics. Success means more creators producing higher-quality content more easily.

title: Product Manager, YouTube Live Living Room
role_summary: PM for YouTube's live streaming experience on TV/living room devices — a consumer video product at the intersection of live content and connected TV.
why_good_fit: Jay has direct live streaming and video product experience from ByteDance (Resso, TikTok) and Meta (content relevance, Shoppertainment which involves live commerce). His understanding of Indian consumer video consumption patterns — especially the shift to living room screens in Tier 2/3 India — is a unique asset. The 3-year minimum experience requirement makes Jay significantly overqualified in a good way, positioning him as a senior hire. His GenZ Product Council work and field research on content consumption behaviours directly inform living room product decisions.
how_to_win: Frame the narrative around understanding how Indian families consume live content on TV — cite field observations from Meta AI field work and Resso user research. Connect Shoppertainment (live commerce) to YouTube Live's monetisation potential on living room devices. Highlight zero-to-one experience (Resso founding team) as evidence of ability to build new product surfaces. Pitch the insight that live content on TV in India has unique social dynamics (family co-viewing, regional language needs) that global product teams miss.
job_description: You'll own the YouTube Live experience on TV and living room devices, defining how live streaming content is discovered, consumed, and interacted with on big screens. Day-to-day involves working with engineering teams to build features for live content on connected TVs, analysing viewer behaviour, and optimising the live viewing experience. Success is measured by live viewership growth, engagement time, and creator adoption of live features on TV surfaces.

title: Product Manager, Google Pay
role_summary: PM for Google Pay — India's leading consumer payments product, serving hundreds of millions of Indian users across UPI, merchant payments, and financial services.
why_good_fit: Google Pay is a quintessential India consumer product serving exactly the user segments Jay excels with: SMBs, informal businesses, Tier 2/3 consumers, and regional users. Jay's SMB enforcement work at Meta (2.3% India revenue uplift, 10M+ businesses impacted) demonstrates deep understanding of how Indian small businesses interact with digital platforms. His bakery-in-Dahod origin story is a perfect Google Pay user story. His Shoppertainment and commerce work shows monetisation thinking relevant to payments. 5-year experience requirement fits perfectly.
how_to_win: Open with the Dahod bakery story — a small business owner whose digital commerce was disrupted by platform enforcement — and connect it directly to Google Pay's mission of enabling digital payments for India's informal economy. Cite the 2.3% revenue uplift and 10M businesses impacted to show scale of SMB impact. Emphasise field research methodology: going to Tier 2/3 India to observe how real users transact. Pitch the insight that Indian SMBs are voice-led and WhatsApp-first — Google Pay needs to meet them where they are. Show AI fluency as relevant for building AI-powered payment experiences.
job_description: You'll own product features within Google Pay, India's massive consumer payments platform. Day-to-day involves defining and shipping payment features for Indian consumers and merchants, working with engineering and design teams, analysing transaction data, and driving adoption metrics. Success means growing active users, transaction volume, and merchant adoption while maintaining trust and safety.

title: Product Manager, eWallets
role_summary: PM for Google's eWallet products — digital wallet infrastructure serving consumer payments, likely with strong India/emerging market focus.
why_good_fit: eWallets in India serve the exact user segments Jay understands deeply: informal SMBs, Tier 2/3 consumers, and users with varying digital literacy. His SMB work at Meta (bakery insight → 10M businesses, 2.3% revenue uplift) shows he can identify product gaps in how platforms serve Indian small businesses. His understanding of voice-led, WhatsApp-first business operations maps to how eWallet users actually behave. 5-year experience requirement is a clean fit. Zero-to-one experience from Resso and his startup is relevant for building new wallet features.
how_to_win: Lead with the SMB enforcement story — demonstrating that global product assumptions about digital commerce don't match Indian reality. Connect this to eWallet design: Indian users need products built for their actual behaviour patterns (voice-led, low digital literacy, informal business operations). Cite field research methodology and 20+ user interviews as evidence of customer discovery capability. Show how Shoppertainment work connects to commerce-enabled wallets.
job_description: You'll own product features for Google's eWallet products, building digital wallet experiences for consumers and merchants. Day-to-day involves defining wallet features, working with payments infrastructure teams, analysing user behaviour, and driving wallet adoption. Success means growing wallet users, increasing transaction frequency, and expanding merchant acceptance.

title: Product Manager, Payments Risk
role_summary: PM for payments risk — building products that detect and prevent fraud/abuse in Google's payment systems while minimising false positives for legitimate users.
why_good_fit: Jay's single most impactful project at Meta was literally about risk/enforcement in payments-adjacent products: the SMB over-enforcement discovery where legitimate Indian businesses were being falsely flagged. He identified that global enforcement models created false positives for Indian SMBs, influenced policy change, and drove creation of an agentic resolution system — resulting in 2.3% India revenue uplift. This is exactly what a Payments Risk PM does: balance fraud prevention with user experience. 3-year minimum experience makes Jay very well-positioned.
how_to_win: This is Jay's strongest story-to-role match. Lead with the full SMB over-enforcement narrative: casual bakery conversation → data analysis revealing false positive patterns → policy change → new product team → agentic resolution system → 10M businesses impacted, 2.3% revenue uplift. Frame it as: 'I've already done payments risk product management — I identified that enforcement models built on global assumptions were destroying legitimate Indian businesses, and I built the case for a fundamentally different approach.' Emphasise the balance between safety and user experience.
job_description: You'll own risk products within Google's payments ecosystem, building systems that detect fraud and abuse while minimising friction for legitimate users. Day-to-day involves defining risk models, working with engineering and data science teams, analysing false positive/negative rates, and shipping features that protect users and merchants. Success means reducing fraud losses while maintaining a smooth payment experience.

title: Product Manager, Payments Platform
role_summary: PM for Google's payments platform infrastructure — the underlying systems powering Google Pay and other payment products.
why_good_fit: Jay's SMB enforcement work (2.3% revenue uplift, 10M businesses) demonstrates understanding of how platform-level decisions impact payment experiences for real users. His experience with Meta's business products (advertising tools, WhatsApp API, Paid Messaging) shows familiarity with platform-level product thinking. The 5-year experience requirement fits. While more infrastructure-focused than Jay's ideal consumer role, the India market context and SMB/merchant focus create strong alignment.
how_to_win: Position as someone who understands the downstream impact of platform decisions on real users — cite the SMB enforcement story where platform-level enforcement rules were destroying Indian businesses. Show that platform PM work requires the same user empathy Jay brings from field research. Emphasise cross-functional leadership experience across product, policy, engineering, and data teams at Meta. Highlight zero-to-one building experience from Resso.
job_description: You'll own components of Google's payments platform, building the infrastructure that powers payment experiences across Google products. Day-to-day involves defining platform capabilities, working with engineering teams on scalability and reliability, and ensuring the platform serves diverse payment use cases. Success means platform adoption by internal product teams, transaction reliability, and enabling new payment experiences.

title: Product Manager, Google Play Apps
role_summary: PM for Google Play's apps ecosystem — managing the app discovery, distribution, and developer experience on Android's primary app marketplace.
why_good_fit: Google Play is a massive consumer platform in India with deep Tier 2/3 penetration — exactly Jay's sweet spot. His experience scaling Resso on Android (India's dominant mobile OS) and understanding of Indian app consumption patterns is directly relevant. His work on content relevance at Meta (recommendation, discovery) maps to app discovery on Play. The 10%/90% content insight from Resso (small content driving most consumption) likely applies to app discovery patterns. 5-year requirement fits perfectly.
how_to_win: Lead with Resso's Android-first India growth story — scaling from alpha to India's largest music streaming app on the same platform (Google Play). Connect the 10%/90% content insight to app discovery: a small number of apps likely drive most installs, creating a cold start problem for new developers. Pitch deep understanding of Indian app consumption patterns across Tier 2/3 users. Show how creator economy experience (TikTok, Meta) translates to developer ecosystem thinking.
job_description: You'll own product features within Google Play's apps experience, shaping how users discover, install, and engage with apps on Android. Day-to-day involves defining app discovery features, working with engineering and design teams, analysing install and engagement data, and partnering with the developer ecosystem. Success means growing app installs, user engagement, and developer satisfaction.

title: Product Manager, AI Gaming, Google Play
role_summary: PM for AI-powered gaming experiences on Google Play — at the intersection of AI product development and consumer gaming on Android.
why_good_fit: This role sits at the intersection of AI product (Jay's top priority) and consumer platform (Google Play). Jay's AI fluency (top 5% Metamate user, personal AI OS, 20+ AI user interviews) combined with his consumer product experience makes this a strong fit. His understanding of Indian gaming consumers (India is one of the largest mobile gaming markets) and Tier 2/3 user behaviour adds unique value. The 5-year requirement with 2 years of 0-to-1 experience maps directly to Jay's Resso founding team and startup experience.
how_to_win: Lead with AI fluency — personal AI OS built on Metamate, top 5% usage, 80% task automation — to demonstrate genuine AI-native thinking. Connect to consumer product experience: Resso's ML recommendation engine (pure ML, no editorial bias) shows understanding of AI-powered consumer experiences. Pitch insights from MetaAI India field work about how Indian users interact with AI — language/tone matters more than factual correctness. Frame gaming as the next frontier for AI-powered consumer experiences in India.
job_description: You'll own AI-powered gaming features on Google Play, defining how AI enhances game discovery, gameplay, and developer tools. Day-to-day involves identifying AI opportunities in gaming, working with engineering and ML teams, shipping AI-powered features, and measuring impact on gaming engagement. Success means growing gaming engagement through AI-powered experiences and enabling developers to leverage AI.

title: Product Manager, Applied AI, Google Cloud
role_summary: PM for Applied AI products within Google Cloud — building GenAI/LLM-powered products for enterprise customers.
why_good_fit: This is an AI-native role (Jay's P0 criterion) at Google, requiring GenAI/LLM domain experience. how_to_win: Lead with the personal AI OS story — built multiple agents on Metamate, automated 80% of tasks, top 5% user globally. Acknowledge the B2B pivot but frame it as: 'I understand how real users adopt AI — from SMBs to enterprises to GenZ consumers — and that user adoption insight is what Applied AI products need.'
job_description: You'll own Applied AI products within Google Cloud, building GenAI and LLM-powered solutions for enterprise customers. Day-to-day involves defining AI product features, working with ML engineering teams, understanding enterprise customer needs, and driving adoption of AI capabilities. Success means enterprise customer adoption, revenue growth, and competitive positioning against other cloud AI offerings.
